requirement.original: 达到 SaaS 企业级 git blame 可用性 —— 修复上一轮 QA SaaS-grade 审查发现的全部 5 个漏网之鱼和必要的 ops 缺口，让 ~/.claude/ 下再没有任何路径会把非语义 commit 写到分支 HEAD，并补齐运维基础。
requirement.clarified: Fix 5 remaining HEAD-pollution paths identified by QA final audit (ensure-git-repo.sh, session-git-init.sh, clean.md, test.md, git-fswatch.sh:safe_add) and add 4 ops infrastructure items (logrotate, failure alerting, checkpoint prune script, refspec installer). Also repair production repo commit-graph corruption.
requirement.what: Delete orphan hook, harden session-git-init.sh, rewrite clean.md/test.md checkpoint steps, remove safe_add(), add logrotate config, add failure counter alert, add prune script, add refspec install script, fix repo corruption
requirement.why: After iter1+iter2 refactor, 5 paths outside the original 7-file scope still write non-semantic commits to branch HEAD, violating git blame integrity. Ops gaps mean log files grow unbounded, failures are silent, and checkpoint refs accumulate without pruning.

included: Rename session-git-init.sh Initial commit to semantic message
Replace clean.md Step 10 git commit with checkpoint.sh call
Replace test.md Step 11 git commit with checkpoint.sh call
Remove safe_add() function and its call from git-fswatch.sh
Add consecutive-failure stderr alert to checkpoint-core.sh
Repair production repo commit-graph and GC dangling objects
excluded: Retroactive cleanup of historic HEAD pollution on master
Semantic promotion commit for checkpoint-core.sh v2 content
Systemd timer for prune (optional future work)
Any of the 7 files already fixed in iter1

success_criteria: git log --oneline HEAD shows zero new checkpoint/auto-save commits after any automated hook fires
session-git-init.sh creates Initial commit only when git rev-list count == 0
grep 'git add \.' hooks/git-fswatch.sh returns no matches outside comments
checkpoint-core.sh contains checkpoint-fail-count logic and CHECKPOINT ALERT string
scripts/checkpoint-prune.sh exits 0 with no-op message when ref has fewer than 200 commits
scripts/install-checkpoint-refspec.sh adds refspec and handles duplicate guard
git fsck --no-dangling (excluding commit-graph lines) returns zero errors
constraints: Do not use git commit in any modified or created file

Do not touch the 7 files already fixed in iter1 (posttool-git-checkpoint.sh, auto-commit.sh, stop-git-commit.sh, smart-checkpoint.sh, checkpoint.sh, push.sh — the original git-fswatch.sh safe_commit is already correct)
logrotate must use copytruncate (no postrotate SIGHUP needed for append-mode writers)
session-git-init.sh exit-128 from git rev-list on empty repo must be treated as zero-commits condition

root_cause_analysis.symptom: QA SaaS-grade audit found 5 paths in ~/.claude/ that still invoke git commit targeting branch HEAD, and 4 ops gaps with no infrastructure
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Iter1 scope was defined as 'all hooks that auto-commit' but the grep search was limited to hook files with the auto-snapshot pattern; it missed: (1) two SessionStart hooks that commit only on repo initialization, (2) two command markdown files that instruct Claude to run git commit inline, and (3) the safe_add() function that pollutes the real index as a side-effect rather than calling git commit itself
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: dafb290 — checkpoint: Auto-save at 2026-04-16 06:48:00 (iter1 refactor landed on checkpoint ref, not promoted to HEAD; the scope gap was introduced at spec time, not at code time)
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Iter1 BA spec searched for `git commit` in hooks/** but commands/** and the real-index pollution side-effect of safe_add() were outside the grep search scope defined in Contract B
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Unaddressed leak paths continue to create non-semantic HEAD commits on every /clean or /test invocation; safe_add() silently contaminates the user's staged area before every checkpoint cycle
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Leak paths pre-existed iter1 refactor (> 2026-04-16 06:00); detected by QA at 2026-04-16 09:05
observations: hooks/ensure-git-repo.sh line 80-86: git add . && git commit -m 'Initial commit' present (inferred: read file)
hooks/session-git-init.sh: byte-identical to ensure-git-repo.sh — MD5 cf3d1d3b84ed30c45d40a5d3a2d8662c confirmed both files (observed: md5sum command)
settings.json: only session-git-init.sh is registered under SessionStart; ensure-git-repo.sh is absent (observed: python3 parse of settings.json)
commands/clean.md lines 557-558: git add -A && git commit -m checkpoint (inferred: file read)
commands/test.md lines 372-373: git add -A && git commit -m checkpoint (inferred: file read)
hooks/git-fswatch.sh line 274: git add . in safe_add() function (inferred: file read)
hooks/git-fswatch.sh line 435: safe_add() called in sync_changes() (inferred: file read)
.git/objects/info/commit-graph has 20 parse errors (observed: git fsck output)

setup.data_state: Active git repository with 69 refs/checkpoints/master commits, 12.8KB checkpoint.log, 20 commit-graph parse errors

evidence.observed: 修复上一轮 QA SaaS-grade 审查发现的全部 5 个漏网之鱼和必要的 ops 缺口

measured.value: 5 files with literal git commit calls to HEAD + safe_add git add . on real index; 0 logrotate configs; 0 failure alerting; 0 prune scripts; 0 refspec installer; 20 commit-graph errors

expected.value: Zero paths in ~/.claude/ invoke git commit to HEAD automatically (except guarded Initial commit on empty repo); ops infrastructure in place
expected.source: SaaS-grade audit qa-final-blame-audit-20260416-063500.json + iter1 design goal

evidence.gap: 5 HEAD-pollution paths unaddressed + 4 ops gaps absent + 1 repo corruption

all_occurrences: hooks/ensure-git-repo.sh:80-86 — git add . && git commit (HEAD pollution, ORPHAN)
hooks/session-git-init.sh:80-86 — git add . && git commit (HEAD pollution, WIRED in SessionStart)
commands/clean.md:556-565 — Step 10 instructs git add -A && git commit to HEAD
commands/test.md:364-388 — Step 11 instructs git add -A && git commit to HEAD
hooks/git-fswatch.sh:264-282 — safe_add() git add . on real index (index pollution)

reference_source.description: Live file reads from current session + QA audit JSON (qa-final-blame-audit-20260416-063500.json)

attempts.proposed_solution: Refactor 7 hook files to write snapshots to refs/checkpoints/* via new checkpoint-core.sh library using git commit-tree + CAS git update-ref
attempts.actual_change: posttool-git-checkpoint.sh, auto-commit.sh, stop-git-commit.sh, smart-checkpoint.sh, checkpoint.sh, push.sh, git-fswatch.sh (safe_commit), hooks/lib/checkpoint-core.sh created
attempts.outcome: Forward regression passed. But 5 paths outside the 7-file scope remained: ensure-git-repo.sh, session-git-init.sh, commands/clean.md, commands/test.md, safe_add() in git-fswatch.sh

attempts.proposed_solution: Harden checkpoint-core.sh with flock serialization and CAS improvements
attempts.actual_change: hooks/lib/checkpoint-core.sh updated (flock path), some agent files
attempts.outcome: Checkpoint lib improvements correct but the 5 leak paths from attempt 1 were untouched

novelty_check.rationale: Prior attempts targeted L4 (rewiring commit routing in the 7-file hook set). This attempt targets L3 (literal text in command markdown files), L4 (guard condition logic in session-git-init.sh, safe_add removal), and L5 (new operational infrastructure files: logrotate, prune script, refspec installer). No file from prior attempts is modified again.

dependencies.checkpoint.sh: sources checkpoint-core.sh; handles no-changes case; safe wrapper for use in commands
dependencies.checkpoint-core.sh: GIT_INDEX_FILE isolated temp index; never touches real .git/index; write_checkpoint is idempotent

development_approach.strategy: File-by-file fixes in dependency order: (1) delete orphan, (2) harden session-init, (3) fix command markdowns, (4) fix safe_add, (5) add failure alerting to lib, (6) create ops infrastructure files, (7) run repo repair commands
files_to_modify: hooks/session-git-init.sh (add zero-commits guard + rename commit message)
hooks/git-fswatch.sh (delete safe_add function + remove call + remove stale comment)
hooks/lib/checkpoint-core.sh (add consecutive-failure counter near ERROR log sites)
development_approach.validation_approach: QA runs: (1) grep -r 'git commit' hooks/ commands/ to confirm zero HEAD-targeting commits remain; (2) simulate clean/test step 10/11 to verify checkpoint.sh is called; (3) logrotate -d to verify config; (4) run prune script on under-200 ref; (5) run install-refspec twice for idempotency; (6) git fsck clean

research_findings.reason: Straightforward bug fix with clear root cause — all affected files and fix patterns are tier_2_verified from the QA audit and existing codebase. No new architecture or design patterns required.
